title: PairwiseMatch v1
description: Record of one judged pairwise comparison between two idea nodes under pre-committed review criteria. The panel is drawn from the third-party agent roster (agents.json): normally one seat per distinct model family, or — when the roster cannot field the cross-family floor — numbered native subagent seats of the host family, declared in panel_independence. Each panel seat votes with anchored arguments only (literature or computation anchors); unanchored arguments are discarded and counted. The outcome is written back to the external belief graph as an observation.

criteria_commitment.description: Review criteria committed before the panel sees the pair, so the criteria cannot be adjusted to fit a preferred outcome.

panel.description: One entry per judge vote. A valid cross-family match holds at least three votes from DISTINCT model families; a valid degraded (single-family) match holds at least three numbered native subagent seats of the one host family. JSON Schema cannot express either distinctness rule, so minItems: 3 here is only the closest schema-expressible floor; the authoritative check on panel composition is the Python validator validate_pairwise_match in skills/idea-pairwise-match/scripts/assemble_match.py.

reviewer_family.description: Model-family label from the third-party agent roster (agents.json) the panel ran under. The label set is roster-defined, not fixed by this schema; family diversity is checked by validate_pairwise_match.

seat.description: Seat number on a degraded single-family (native subagent) panel; absent on cross-family panels. Seat distinctness is checked by validate_pairwise_match.

unanchored_arguments_discarded.description: Count of arguments this seat produced that carried no literature/computation anchor and were therefore discarded before voting.

observation_write.description: Whether the match outcome has been written to the external belief graph as an observation.

gaia_package_ref.description: Optional reference to the belief-graph package that received the observation.

panel_independence.description: Panel composition record, read from the panel run report at assembly time: whether the panel was cross-family (distinct roster families) or single-family (native subagent seats, after the roster could not field the cross-family floor), which families voted, and which requested families were absent with the recorded reason. Consistency with the panel array is checked by validate_pairwise_match.

independent_runners.description: Whether the panel seats resolved to genuinely distinct underlying runner commands. False marks a stub-backed, single-command, or degraded native subagent panel whose votes carry no real cross-family diversity, so the belief layer can weight the observation accordingly. Read from the panel run report at assembly time.

statement_binding.description: Per-side binding of the advocacy statement the judges saw: the arguing node id and the sha256 of the statement content. Written when --materials-dir is supplied at assembly (required in a real match); validated by validate_pairwise_match against the recorded node ids.

rationale.description: Optional operator-supplied reason for a rematch of the same idea pair, recorded so a repeat observation carries its stated new-evidence justification.
